1→// ============================================================================
     2→// TOOL EXECUTOR FRAMEWORK
     3→// Handles registration and execution of tool handlers
     4→// Related: ADR 0009 - Tool Execution Architecture
     5→// ============================================================================
     6→
     7→import { nanoid } from 'nanoid';
     8→import { broadcastToolEvent } from '@/lib/realtime/tool-events';
     9→import type { ToolType, ToolExecutionResult, ToolContext } from '@/types/tools';
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Tool handler function signature
    13→ */
    14→type ToolHandler = (
    15→  args: Record<string, unknown>,
    16→  context: ToolContext
    17→) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult>;
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Registry of tool handlers
    21→ */
    22→const handlers = new Map<string, ToolHandler>();
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Register a tool handler for a function name
    26→ * @param functionName - OpenAI function name (e.g., 'create_mindmap')
    27→ * @param handler - Async function that executes the tool
    28→ */
    29→export function registerToolHandler(
    30→  functionName: string,
    31→  handler: ToolHandler
    32→): void {
    33→  handlers.set(functionName, handler);
    34→}
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Get all registered handlers (for testing)
    38→ */
    39→export function getRegisteredHandlers(): Map<string, ToolHandler> {
    40→  return new Map(handlers);
    41→}
    42→
    43→/**
    44→ * Clear all handlers (for testing)
    45→ */
    46→export function clearHandlers(): void {
    47→  handlers.clear();
    48→}
    49→
    50→/**
    51→ * Map function names to tool types
    52→ */
    53→function getToolTypeFromFunctionName(functionName: string): ToolType {
    54→  const mapping: Record<string, ToolType> = {
    55→    create_mindmap: 'mindmap',
    56→    create_quiz: 'quiz',
    57→    create_demo: 'demo',
    58→    web_search: 'search',
    59→    create_flashcards: 'flashcard',
    60→    create_diagram: 'diagram',
    61→    create_timeline: 'timeline',
    62→    create_summary: 'summary',
    63→    create_formula: 'formula',
    64→    create_chart: 'chart',
    65→  };
    66→  return mapping[functionName] || 'mindmap';
    67→}
    68→
    69→/**
    70→ * Execute a tool call
    71→ * @param functionName - Name of the function to call (from OpenAI tool_calls)
    72→ * @param args - Arguments from the function call
    73→ * @param context - Session context (sessionId, userId, maestroId)
    74→ */
    75→export async function executeToolCall(
    76→  functionName: string,
    77→  args: Record<string, unknown>,
    78→  context: ToolContext
    79→): Promise<ToolExecutionResult> {
    80→  const handler = handlers.get(functionName);

content.text:
Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the summary tool implementation. Let me create a detailed report. ## Summary Tool Implementation Report I've found and analyzed all the key files related to the summary tool in the ConvergioEdu codebase. Here's a complete breakdown: ### 1. Component Showing "In attesa dello strumento..." **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/focus-tool-layout.tsx` (line 419) This message appears in the **right panel** of the focus tool layout when no tool has been created yet: ```typescript // Lines 414-422

In attesa dello strumento...

Parla con {characterProps?.name || 'il coach'} per creare il contenuto

``` The layout has: - Left side (1/3): Chat interface with maestro/coach - Right side (2/3): Tool panel that shows this message until a tool is created --- ### 2. Summary Tool Components #### Main Summary Tool Component **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/summary-tool.tsx` - Wrapper component for displaying summaries in read/edit mode - Handles title and section editing - Provides action buttons: Edit, Save, PDF Export, Convert to Mindmap, Generate Flashcards - Props accept callbacks for data changes, exports, and conversions #### Live Summary Component **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/live-summary.tsx` - Real-time summary editor with SSE event listening - Listens to voice command modifications via `useSummaryModifications` hook - Displays connection status (green "Connesso - modifica vocale attiva" or amber "Connessione in corso...") - Supports inline real-time updates from maestro #### Student Summary Editor **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/student-summary-editor.tsx` - Maieutic method: student writes their own summary - 3-section guided structure: Introduzione, Sviluppo, Conclusione - Maestro can add inline comments with feedback - Word count tracking - Saves to materials archive via `/api/tools/saved` --- ### 3. Tool Handler (Backend Processing) **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/summary-handler.ts` Registers two tool handlers: 1. **`create_summary`** - AI-generated summaries - Validates topic and sections structure - Normalizes section data (title, content, keyPoints) - Returns structured `SummaryData` 2. **`open_student_summary`** - Student-written summaries - Opens empty editor with guided structure - Creates `StudentSummaryData` with 3 sections and guiding questions - Supports maestro feedback via `student_summary_add_comment` handler --- ### 4. API Routes #### Chat API (Tool Invocation) **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/chat/route.ts` Flow: 1. Accepts `requestedTool: 'summary'` parameter 2. Injects tool context into system prompt (lines 83-91): ```typescript summary: `STAI CREANDO UN RIASSUNTO STRUTTURATO con lo studente. Linee guida: - Chiedi prima quale argomento vuole riassumere - Organizza il contenuto in sezioni chiare e logiche - Usa create_summary per costruire il riassunto - Includi punti chiave per ogni sezione - Adatta la lunghezza alle esigenze dello studente (breve/medio/lungo) - Fai domande per approfondire i punti importanti` ``` 3. Executes `executeToolCall` with function name `create_summary` 4. Returns tool results to client #### Conversation Summarization API **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/conversations/[id]/summarize/route.ts` - Triggered when conversation gets too long (>20 messages) - Keeps last 10 messages, summarizes older ones - Extracts key facts, topics, and learnings in parallel - Updates conversation record with summary - Saves learnings to database for personalization --- ### 5. AI/LLM Layer **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/summarize.ts` LLM functions for conversation analysis: - `generateConversationSummary()` - Creates concise summary (max 200 words, Italian) - `extractKeyFacts()` - JSON: decisions, preferences, learned - `extractTopics()` - Returns array of main topics discussed - `extractLearnings()` - Extracts student insights (preference, strength, weakness, interest, style) --- ### 6. Session Summary Generation **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/conversation/summary-generator.ts` Called when conversation ends: - `endConversationWithSummary()` - Generates summary for closed conversations - `getLastConversationSummary()` - Retrieves recent summary for contextual greetings - `getRecentSummaries()` - Dashboard overview of last 5 summaries --- ### 7. Type Definitions **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/types/tools.ts` (lines 467-573) ```typescript // AI-generated summary structure interface SummaryData { topic: string; sections: SummarySection[]; length?: 'short' | 'medium' | 'long'; } interface SummarySection { title: string; content: string; keyPoints?: string[]; } // Student-written summary structure interface StudentSummaryData { id: string; title: string; topic: string; sections: StudentSummarySection[]; // Each with guidingQuestion + comments wordCount: number; createdAt: Date; lastModifiedAt: Date; maestroId?: string; sessionId?: string; } ``` --- ### 8. Real-Time Modifications (Voice Commands) **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/hooks/use-summary-modifications.ts` Supports SSE events for real-time voice-command modifications: - `summary_set_title` - Change title - `summary_add_section` - Add new section - `summary_update_section` - Modify section content - `summary_delete_section` - Remove section - `summary_add_point` - Add key point - `summary_delete_point` - Remove key point - `summary_finalize` - Mark as complete --- ### 9. Tool Execution Framework **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/tool-executor.ts` Registry-based handler system: - `registerToolHandler()` - Register handlers for function names - `executeToolCall()` - Execute registered handler - Maps function names to tool types (e.g., `create_summary` → `summary`) --- ### 10. Tool Panel Display **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/tool-panel.tsx` Renders based on tool type: - For `summary` type: Renders `SummaryTool` component - For `student_summary` type: Renders `StudentSummaryEditor` with save to materials --- ### 11. Chat Tool Definitions **File**: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/types/tools.ts` (lines 196-233) Two summary function definitions for OpenAI: 1. **`create_summary`** - AI generates summary 2. **`open_student_summary`** - Opens editor for student to write --- ## Invocation Flow ``` 1. User in chat: "Fammi un riassunto di..." ↓ 2. Chat API (/api/chat) receives message with requestedTool: 'summary' ↓ 3. System prompt injected with summary context ↓ 4. Azure OpenAI/Ollama calls create_summary function ↓ 5. executeToolCall → summary-handler.ts ↓ 6. Handler validates sections and returns SummaryData ↓ 7. ToolPanel renders SummaryTool with data ↓ 8.
